title: Moisture Sensors & Their Applications in Urology: A Review
author: Sampoorn Naik
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Diapers are used to prevent   involuntary flow of urine among people   suffering   from   incontinence.   For   bedridden   patients   who   cannot   communicate, the diapers become soaked   in moisture before the attendant is aware   of the situation. Excess moisture leads to    fungal    infections   and   bedsores,   which   may   result in associated infections. To address   these challenges, the paper explores the   potential   of   moisture   sensors   incorporated   into   wearable   devices,   such   as   smart   diapers, adhesive patches, or bed sheets.   These   sensors   enable   continuous   monitoring of moisture levels, providing   real-time data for early intervention and   targeted treatment   strategies. The paper   highlights   the   available   design   considerations and technology of various   sensors,   emphasizing   the   potential   to   enhance   patient   care   and   improve   outcomes   in   managing   the   health   problems   arising from moist diapers.

keywords: Sensors, Diaper, PEDOT:PSS, incontinence, Interdigitated electrodes, flexible
